title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction · Scale Transfer
summary: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.
Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
